_comment: Structured rubric distilled from job_description.docx (Senior AI Engineer, Founding Team, Redrob AI). Used by features.py and rank.py to score candidates.jsonl. Plain-English JD requirements were translated into checkable signals over candidate_schema.json fields.

experience_band._comment: JD: '5-9 years... ideal candidate ~6-8'. Soft falloff outside acceptable range, never a hard cutoff -- 'we'll seriously consider candidates outside the band if other signals are strong'.

title_relevance._comment: Title match is ONE signal, not a filter -- JD explicitly says a 'Tier 5' candidate without an AI title but with relevant production career history (caught via semantic similarity over career_history descriptions) must also score well.

skills._comment_weak_signal: JD: 'AI experience consisting primarily of recent (<12mo) LangChain-calls-OpenAI projects' is a soft red flag UNLESS backed by older/deeper IR or ML skills.

anti_stuffing._comment: A skill listed with 0 endorsements and <=1 month duration counts at ~30% weight -- catches keyword-stuffer trap candidates whose skill list looks perfect but is unsubstantiated.
anti_stuffing._comment_assessment: redrob_signals.skill_assessment_scores gives a 0-100 platform-administered test score per skill (~25% of candidates have 1-2 scored skills, observed avg ~53). A skill with neither endorsements nor tenure but an assessment score >= 60 is upgraded from 30% to 75% credit -- a keyword-stuffer cannot fake a proctored test, and this specifically helps genuine 'Tier 5 plain-language' candidates whose real competency hasn't yet accrued endorsements/duration on the platform. An 'expert'-claimed skill with an assessment score < 35 is surfaced as corroborating detail in honeypot reasoning (not an independent trigger, to avoid false positives from one noisy test).

disqualifier_industries._comment_consulting: JD: reject candidates whose ENTIRE career has been at consulting-style firms (proxy: every career_history entry has industry=='IT Services'). A candidate currently at such a firm but with prior product-company experience is fine.

location._comment: JD: Pune/Noida preferred; Hyderabad, Pune, Mumbai, Delhi NCR 'welcome to apply'. Bangalore/Bengaluru added as a major Indian tech hub consistent with 'Tier-1 Indian cities'.
location._comment_outside: JD: 'Outside India: case-by-case, but we don't sponsor work visas.' Heavily down-weighted unless willing_to_relocate.

notice_period._comment: JD: 'sub-30-day notice' loved, can buy out up to 30 days (so <=60 effectively coverable), '30+ day notice candidates still in scope but bar gets higher'.

behavioral_signals._comment: JD final note: 'a perfect-on-paper candidate who hasn't logged in for 6 months and has a 5% recruiter response rate is, for hiring purposes, not actually available. Down-weight them appropriately.' Implemented as a multiplier in [behavioral.min_multiplier, behavioral.max_multiplier].

honeypot_heuristics._comment: Profiles with >=3 'expert' skills that have 0 endorsements AND 0 duration_months, OR whose summed career_history duration wildly exceeds stated years_of_experience, are flagged as honeypot-suspects and forced to near-zero score.
